children.text: It doesn't knock, Just slips the lock, A quiet, creeping gray. No warning sign, It crosses the line, And steals the light of day.
It settles deep, While I’m asleep, Or mid-day, sunny bright. A heavy weight, That seals my fate, And turns the noon to night.
No loud demand, Just takes my hand, And leads me to the shade. It breaks the spell, Where I was well, And leaves me, unafraid.
it doesn’t knock, Just stops the clock, And stays, for now, to stay. A silent guest, Upon my chest, That takes my breath away.
